title: Grade 7 General Science — Units 4-7
description: 50 challenging questions covering cells, living things diversity, Earth in space, and motion/force/energy.

questions.question: A student uses a compound microscope with a 10X eyepiece and wants a total magnification of 400X. Which objective lens should they select?

Robert Hooke observed cork cells in 1665 using a compound microscope, but Anton van Leeuwenhoek is considered the founder of microscopy. What key advantage did Leeuwenhoek's simple microscopes have over Hooke's compound microscope?
options: They could magnify up to 300X without blurring
They used multiple lenses for higher resolution
They were the first to use oil immersion lenses
They could observe multicellular organisms in color
questions.correctAnswer: They could magnify up to 300X without blurring

questions.question: A unicellular organism is observed under a microscope. It is green, contains chloroplasts, and moves toward light. Into which kingdom should this organism be classified?
questions.correctAnswer: Protista

questions.question: During complete aerobic respiration, one glucose molecule produces up to 38 ATP. If a cell breaks down 3 glucose molecules entirely through aerobic respiration, how many ATP molecules would be produced in total?
An organism is eukaryotic, multicellular, has a cell wall made of cellulose, contains chloroplasts, and is non-motile. This organism belongs to kingdom:
questions.correctAnswer: Plantae

questions.question: A cell has a cell wall made of cellulose, a large central vacuole, and chloroplasts. Based on these features, which organelle would this cell most likely lack?
questions.correctAnswer: Lysosomes

questions.question: Anaerobic respiration in yeast produces ethanol and carbon dioxide, while anaerobic respiration in animal cells produces lactic acid. Both processes share which common outcome?
options: They both produce 38 ATP per glucose molecule
They both produce only 2 ATP per glucose molecule
They both require oxygen to proceed
They both produce water as a byproduct
questions.correctAnswer: They both produce only 2 ATP per glucose molecule

questions.question: A student views a specimen using the 10X eyepiece and the 40X objective, but the image is blurry when using the coarse adjustment knob. To obtain a sharp focus under high power, the student should use the:
questions.correctAnswer: Fine adjustment knob only

questions.question: A plant has rectangular, rigid cells with distinct edges, while an animal has irregular-shaped cells. The primary reason for this difference is that plant cells have:
options: A larger nucleus that maintains shape
A cell wall that forces a definite shape
A fluid-filled vacuole that fills the cell
questions.correctAnswer: A cell wall that forces a definite shape

questions.question: The human egg cell measures approximately 0.12 mm in diameter, while the bacterium Mycoplasma gallicepticum measures about 0.0001 mm. The egg cell is larger because:
options: It stores more genetic material than bacteria
It needs extra energy reserves for zygote development
It contains multiple nuclei for rapid division
It has a thicker cell wall for protection
questions.correctAnswer: It needs extra energy reserves for zygote development

questions.question: Carl Linnaeus developed binomial nomenclature. Two organisms share the same genus name but have different species names. What can you conclude about these two organisms?
options: They are closely related but cannot produce fertile offspring together
They can interbreed to produce fertile offspring
They belong to the same phylum but different classes
questions.correctAnswer: They are closely related but cannot produce fertile offspring together

questions.question: A horse and a donkey can mate and produce a mule, but the mule is infertile. In terms of biological classification, this is because horses and donkeys:
options: Belong to the same species but different genera
Belong to the same genus but different species
Belong to different classes within the same phylum
Belong to the same kingdom but different phyla
questions.correctAnswer: Belong to the same genus but different species

questions.question: Which sequence correctly shows Linnaeus's classification hierarchy from the broadest group to the most specific group?
options: Species, Genus, Family, Order, Class, Phylum, Kingdom
Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Kingdom, Class, Phylum, Order, Family, Genus, Species
Phylum, Kingdom, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
questions.correctAnswer: Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species

questions.question: You discover a new organism that is eukaryotic, unicellular, and contains chloroplasts for photosynthesis. Under the five-kingdom system, it should be classified under:
questions.correctAnswer: Protista

questions.question: A mushroom, a yeast cell, and bread mold are all classified in Kingdom Fungi. Which characteristic do all members of this kingdom share?
options: They are autotrophic and make their own food
They have cell walls made of chitin and are heterotrophic
They are prokaryotic and reproduce by binary fission
They store food as starch and have cellulose cell walls
questions.correctAnswer: They have cell walls made of chitin and are heterotrophic

questions.question: Blue-green algae (cyanobacteria) perform photosynthesis but are classified under Kingdom Monera rather than Plantae. What is the primary reason?
options: They lack chlorophyll and use a different pigment
They are prokaryotic and lack a true nucleus
They are multicellular and form colonies
They have cell walls made of cellulose
questions.correctAnswer: They are prokaryotic and lack a true nucleus

questions.question: A scientist classifies organisms by considering internal features, external features, and evolutionary relationships. This method of classification is known as:
questions.correctAnswer: Natural or biological classification

questions.question: An insect has a body divided into three parts and three pairs of legs. According to the text, which additional feature do insects typically possess?
questions.correctAnswer: Two pairs of wings

questions.question: Mosses and ferns both reproduce by spores, but ferns are vascular plants while mosses are not. Which statement correctly describes this distinction?
options: Ferns have well-developed xylem and phloem; mosses lack vascular tissue
Mosses have true roots and stems; ferns have only rhizomes
Ferns produce seeds; mosses produce spores
Mosses grow only in water; ferns grow only on land
questions.correctAnswer: Ferns have well-developed xylem and phloem; mosses lack vascular tissue

questions.question: Fish are poikilothermic vertebrates that live only in water. Which combination of features is characteristic of fish?
options: Moist skin, lungs, and four limbs
Scales, gills, fins, and external fertilization
Dry scales, lungs, and eggs with rubbery shells
questions.correctAnswer: Scales, gills, fins, and external fertilization

questions.question: The scientific name for humans is Homo sapiens. According to the rules of binomial nomenclature, the first part of this name (Homo) represents the:
questions.correctAnswer: Genus

questions.question: Amphibians live both in water and on land, but they are not fully adapted to terrestrial life. Which feature supports this statement?
options: They have dry skin with scales to prevent water loss
They lay jelly-covered eggs in water and have moist skin
They breathe only through gills throughout their life
They produce eggs with hard waterproof shells on land
questions.correctAnswer: They lay jelly-covered eggs in water and have moist skin

questions.question: Gymnosperms are called 'naked seed' plants because:
options: They produce flowers but no seeds
Their seeds are not enclosed in a fruit
They lack vascular tissue for transport
questions.correctAnswer: Their seeds are not enclosed in a fruit

questions.question: Birds and mammals are both homoeothermic, but they differ in reproduction. Which statement correctly distinguishes them?
options: Birds give birth to live young; mammals lay eggs
Birds lay eggs with hard shells; mammals produce live young
Birds have mammary glands; mammals have feathers
Birds are cold-blooded; mammals are warm-blooded
questions.correctAnswer: Birds lay eggs with hard shells; mammals produce live young

questions.question: Protists can be classified as animal-like or plant-like. Which feature distinguishes plant-like protists (protophyta) from animal-like protists (protozoans)?
options: They are multicellular while protozoans are unicellular
They have cell walls and chloroplasts for photosynthesis
They reproduce only sexually while protozoans reproduce asexually
They are prokaryotic while protozoans are eukaryotic
questions.correctAnswer: They have cell walls and chloroplasts for photosynthesis

questions.question: Ancient Greek philosopher Aristotle (384-322 B.C.) used physical evidence to argue for a spherical Earth. Which two observations did he specifically cite?
options: Ships disappearing hull-first and different constellations at different latitudes
Sunrise and sunset times and satellite images
Circumnavigation and the curved horizon from mountains
questions.correctAnswer: Ships disappearing hull-first and different constellations at different latitudes

questions.question: The equatorial circumference of Earth is 40,075 km, but the polar circumference is only 40,008 km. This difference is best explained by:
options: The gravitational pull of the moon stretching the equator
Earth's rotation causing an equatorial bulge
Continental drift pushing material toward the equator
The tilt of Earth's axis compressing the poles
questions.correctAnswer: Earth's rotation causing an equatorial bulge

questions.question: An airplane is cruising at an altitude of 12 km near the equator. Based on the text, which atmospheric layer is the airplane flying in?
questions.correctAnswer: Troposphere

questions.question: Ozone in the stratosphere absorbs ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the sun. According to the text, what direct health benefit does this provide?
options: It warms the Earth's surface for agriculture
It protects us from skin cancer and other health damage
It prevents acid rain from forming
It increases oxygen levels in the troposphere
questions.correctAnswer: It protects us from skin cancer and other health damage

questions.question: Two ships are approaching an observer along the same line of sight, maintaining a considerable distance between them. If the Earth's surface were flat, the observer would see:
options: Only the front ship because it blocks the back ship
Both ships at the same time
The back ship before the front ship
Neither ship until they are very close
questions.correctAnswer: Both ships at the same time

questions.question: In the nitrogen cycle, which step is the only one NOT carried out by bacteria?
questions.correctAnswer: Assimilation

questions.question: During a lunar eclipse, Earth's shadow on the moon is always circular. What does this phenomenon demonstrate about Earth?
options: Earth is rotating on its axis
Earth is closer to the moon than the sun
Earth's atmosphere bends light around it
questions.correctAnswer: Earth has a spherical shape

questions.question: Earth's average distance from the sun is defined as one astronomical unit (AU). Approximately what is this distance?
The Earth rotates from west to east. If it is currently noon in a city located at 45° East longitude, what time is it at the Prime Meridian (0° longitude)?
questions.correctAnswer: 9:00 AM the same day

questions.question: In the water cycle, water returns to the atmosphere as vapor through two main processes. These are:
questions.correctAnswer: Evaporation and transpiration

questions.question: During the Northern Hemisphere summer (June to August), the North Pole points toward the sun. What season is occurring in the Southern Hemisphere during these months?
questions.correctAnswer: Winter

questions.question: The crust is the thinnest layer of Earth. Which statement correctly compares oceanic and continental crust?
options: Oceanic crust is thicker and made of granite
Continental crust is denser and made of basalt
Oceanic crust is denser and thinner than continental crust
Both types of crust have the same thickness and composition
questions.correctAnswer: Oceanic crust is denser and thinner than continental crust

questions.question: A ball thrown horizontally from a height follows a curved path as it falls toward the ground. According to the text, this type of motion is called:
questions.correctAnswer: Curvilinear motion

questions.question: A soccer player kicks a stationary ball, causing it to move. Later, the same player kicks a moving ball at an angle, changing its direction. Which two effects of force are demonstrated?
options: Change of shape and change of speed
Move a stationary object and change direction of a moving object
Stop a moving object and change its shape
Change speed and stop a moving object
questions.correctAnswer: Move a stationary object and change direction of a moving object

questions.question: Before using a spring balance (Newton meter) to measure force, what must the user do first?
options: Oil the spring to reduce friction
Check that the force meter reads zero
Attach the hook to the heaviest object available
Warm the spring to room temperature
questions.correctAnswer: Check that the force meter reads zero

questions.question: According to the text, which transducer converts chemical energy into electrical energy?
questions.correctAnswer: Battery

questions.question: A student squeezes a toothpaste tube, changing its shape, and toothpaste is forced out through the opening. This action demonstrates which effects of force?
options: Change speed and stop a moving object
Change shape and move a stationary object
Start a stationary object and stop a moving object
questions.correctAnswer: Change shape and move a stationary object

questions.question: Non-renewable energy sources are considered more harmful to the environment than renewable sources primarily because:
options: They are more expensive to extract and use
They have a comparatively higher carbon footprint and carbon emissions
They produce only heat and no usable electricity
questions.correctAnswer: They have a comparatively higher carbon footprint and carbon emissions

questions.question: A spinning top rotates on its own axis, while a simple pendulum swings back and forth. These are examples of which two types of motion, respectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Rotary and oscillatory

questions.question: According to the text, which device transforms mechanical energy into electrical energy?
questions.correctAnswer: Generator

questions.question: The text lists three R's for energy conservation. Which of the three is described as 'the best way to conserve natural resources and reduce pollution'?
questions.correctAnswer: Reduce

questions.question: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, but it can be converted from one form to another. A device that transforms energy from one form to another is called a:
questions.correctAnswer: Transducer

questions.question: Gravitational force is classified as a non-contact force. Which other forces are also classified as non-contact forces in the text?
questions.correctAnswer: Magnetic and electrical forces

questions.question: Both resource depletion and environmental degradation are caused by human activities. Which factor is listed in the text as a cause of BOTH resource depletion and environmental degradation?
questions.correctAnswer: Deforestation

questions.question: A car moving along a straight road speeds up when the driver presses the accelerator and slows down when the driver applies the brakes. According to the text, these actions demonstrate that force can:
options: Move a stationary object and change its shape
Change the speed of a moving object and stop a moving object
Change direction and change the shape of an object
Start a stationary object and change its direction
questions.correctAnswer: Change the speed of a moving object and stop a moving object
